reviews.review_msg: Raegan was the TA for my class.  She was amazing! Extremely helpful and makes it easy to participate (even when it was SUPER early).  She always responded to emails so quickly which was awesome.  I would DEFINITELY take a class with her!! Better than most profs with PHD's! Take her class(es)!!!!

Tony is great if you are genuinely interested in the legal profession, or like a good story, but his teaching skills are seriously lacking. He is unorganized with his notes and the midterm exam was long and unfair. Interesting guy but would not recommend him.

Tony is an alright prof.  Hes not super clear, but he definitely knows what hes talking about.  Make no mistake, this is a VERY hard class (especially for 100 level).  You need to know everything to do well.  However, you don't need the textbook too much, as everything on the exam is covered on the in class notes.  Just be prepared to write a lot!

Awful prof and class. He talks too fast, covers too much material, and when asked to clarify just makes it worse. He makes rude comments and bad jokes. Told us this is his hobby since he make so much during his day job. Awful midterm. Too many questions and too much expect from 1st yrs. My TA (Sarah) is the only part I like about this class.

Unreasonable hard class. Said that students told him he was too easy last year so he's making it harder now. Midterm was 30 short answer questions and 2 essay to be completed in 2 hours. Completely unreasonable. Courtroom assignment is 2,000 words (longer than most CRIM term papers) yet only worth 10%? Just avoid this class
Took him in Fall 2012. A little disorganized but overall a great prof. The course wasn't overly difficult or time consuming, only had two exams and no essays.

I'm a bit biased because I enjoyed the course. Feel like it's a bit unfair to expect first year students to write two assigns at about 14 double spaced pages in a first year course. The 1st assign (of 4) in the course I hated SO MUCH but the course I ended up really enjoying! Too bad we couldn't pick the topics to write about either.
Took this online. 4 paper + online discussion  100% of course grade. Tons of readings and its very confusing. NEVER TAKE IT ONLINE.
Quite a bit of readings, midterm and final are all essay questions. Prof has no lec notes but I would recommend taking a class with him. Overall the class wasnt too difficult.

The textbook that he wrote was actually semi-interesting but the way he books it out of his class the second it's time and is just generally dismissive is so irritating. There are no notes during class and the amount of readings we have to do is absolutely ridiculous.

I took this class via distance ed. There is a lot of emphasis on interpretation of the readings and case studies, rather than straight memorization. No midterm or final for this course, just 4 term papers so there is a lot of reading/writing.
Biggest waste of everyone's time, money and energy! Regretted taking the course! It was at 8:30, it was boring and i felt like leaving every class. Avoid her, if she is still teaching.
Her MT was super easy....15 questions, 10 of them were M/C. Her Final was Open Book...i expected it to be just as easy..YA right.. it was waaay harder..holy eff it screwed me. She's really nice.
He's difinitely a good TA n knows what he's talking about! Also he's really cute!!

Amazing prof for this course! Lectures engaging, content pretty clear and interesting. Allows cheat sheets for tests ( midterm and final ) and tutorials were very chill with one presentation that's 5 minutes only - don't even have to present - you can upload audio of you talking. Would definitely recommend !
She is alright, her midterms and final is with notes but the assignments are graded hard. I would rate higher but she does not post her slides post or pre lecture.

Tamara is a great prof, she gives great lectures and makes the course content interesting. Assignment and exam grading is pretty logical and straight forward. Theres a mandatory tutorial section with participation factored into the final grade, and youre expected to read the textbook before lecture (about 30-40 pages a week).
Not a prof/instructor, but she was a TA for CRIM135. She is funny and very chill. She is very passionate about restorative justice.
Sweet person. You can feel she is helping you. Answer my questions and listen to my concerns.  She is a nice person
Amy doesn't grade assignments. The marking is her TA, who definitely had preferences for types of student
no.. just no..

I have never, in my entire time at SFU, received a grade this low but I've just accepted it because I'm just glad to not be in her class anymore. She is so unfair with tests and genuinely sets you up for failure. Be ready to memorize the whole textbook word for word or you will fail. Honestly, if you don't have to take her class, DONT.

Don't let the reviews scare you. The bad reviews are left by ppl who don't put in the time to take notes, do the readings, and study. I'm not sure about her upper courses but 135 is not bad at all. I appreciate that there isn't just a midterm and final, she incorporates multiple small quizzes. The class is hard but she tells you how to succeed.
Lecture "notes" are just photos with little to none context... she's a tough grader when it comes to written assignments and the written portions of exams/midterms. Wouldn't recommend at all..
Worst prof ever. Run as far as you can because she will genuinely make you contemplate living.
She almost made me want to drop out

Amy Conroy is the most frustrating professor I've had at SFU. The course feels disorganized, and communication is unclear. Our midterm grades were released on March 9, even though the drop deadline was March 8. Students were required to stay in the course without seeing their results, and it appears the grades were returned late on purpose.
I literally had to drop this class first lecture because it was so bad. She told the whole lecture hall to not expect to get an A in this class. I said I'm OUT.

With all the efforts put into reading, I still found the grading criteria for reflection assignments to be unclear and impossibly strict at times. Nevertheless, Amy did an exceptional job in structuring the course in a way that forces you to learn and deserve the grade. Not easy at all, but it was a very, very useful breadth elective.

Read the textbook, pay attention during lecture. She will often go off topic during lecture, but somehow the subjects she brushes over will still end up being on the exam. I didn't find her to be as bad as the ratings might suggest, but if you really hate reading then I wouldn't take this course with her. Fairly reasonable with marks at least.

I think Amy is an incredibly reasonable prof, as long as you actually read the textbook and apply yourself you will be fine. You have to study and it's not an easy pass but I think the class was interesting and the grading was fair but tough. The assignments and quizzes are easy as long as you prep and she drops your lowest grade.

her lectures are great if you are interested in the subject, but they are misleading because her exam questions make absolutely zero sense and have no correlation to what she talks about. the cheat sheets are a SCAM

I like her lectures, but she often loses her train of thought. For example, on the final I recognized a handful of questions that she "went over" in class, but I couldn't remember the answer because she lost her train of thought and never got around to answering the question itself. Cheat sheets are unhelpful because exam questions are strategic

I don't understand how this is a first-level university course. On her midterm, there was a question about a case that was only briefly mentioned and not memorable at all. The question only had the case name. If you want to get an A in this course, you basically have to memorize the entire book front to back. I don't recommend her class to anyone.

She bases all of her teachings on the textbook and expects you to memorize the whole book. Her tests arent easy and she expects you to know every detail of a case while theres hundreds of cases. Shes not understanding when you ask her for help. Take any other professor

Avoid this woman like the plague. She expects you to memorize the entire textbook and over a hundred cases. She acknowledged that her midterm was unfair and received many complaints about her teaching style, but said word for word, "I'm not going to change how I teach or how I make my exams". Lose the God complex, Amy. It's not a good colour on you

yo idk wtf the people in these ratings were doing all semester. ya the class is tough but if u do the readings ur gonna be fine. also for the tests she literally tells u which case to memorize and then all u have to do is write out the facts of the case and stuff and answer one questions. tough exams but u get cheat sheet. prepare & you'll be fine

when I first read the reviews for her I genuinely thought I would fail. its clear that those who are complaining didn't listen to Amy when she told you how to do good in the class. do the readings (like actually) and you will do good. the in-class memos did NOT have hard instructions and were not confusing at all so idk why people are saying that

If you want to take this class you have to know everything from the textbook like the back of your hand. If on an assignment or test you write a summary for a case you memorized from google instead of the textbook she will mark it wrong. She includes random questions she asks during lectures on the midterm so don't miss them.

Not sure why the other ratings on here are so bad. Professor Conroy is a passionate prof who actually speaks TO the class instead of reading off slides like most other profs. I agree that in-class assignments are graded harshly, but textbook is easy to understand unlike most others. She is very clear about the course learning objectives/review.

Amy was a good lecturer, though her slides only contained a few words, so pay very close attention to what she is saying and take clear notes. We were allowed cheat sheets for the exams and they were not super difficult exams. The in-class assignments were marked very harshly and the instructions on how to do better for the next one were not clear.

Dr. Conroy is extremely passionate, not only about criminology, but also about teaching. She gives crystal clear guidelines for how to succeed and numerous opportunities to practice core skills before graded assignments. I never felt like I had to teach myself from the textbook. Her lectures reinforced the textbook and included practice activities.

Grading is so harsh. 2-page cheat sheet allowed for the midterm/final, and the avg was 65% for both. First year course btw lol. Read the textbook line by line, you won't pass if you don't. The lecture was useless, stick to the textbook, only go for the in-class assignments and quizzes.

Worst prof ever her slides don't even have anything on them in lectures she just talks on and onn without like even covering the key concepts in the course. She gives in class assignments which she marks very harshly and her quizzes are in person. Tests are very specific and long.

Her lectures are completely useless and I think she free balls them. Can only get a good grade if you memorize the textbook like it's the bible. Every written assignment will be based off aboriginal people. Should definitely not be teaching and probably shouldn't be incorporating her own political beliefs into the course as nobody is there for her!

I've never known a teacher who talks SO FAST???? She rambles and just glazes over the important topics. She's SO rushed. Most of my classes you hear keyboards typing away for notes at any given time but her classes I've heard maybe 5 students type something at some points of the class and the slides are not helpful at all.

DO NOT take this class for credits. Her lectures do not contain much material and she relies on the textbook to teach you. She would quite often go on tangents about things unrelated to the subject at hand.
On top of that after the final she refused to post my mark and marked me as N until I met with her over zoom even into the following semester.

I'm not sure why the other reviews on here are so bad. Amy was a good prof who gave decent lectures. People who did bad in the course must not have paid attention/read the textbook because the exams were mainly that based on that The in-class assignments were also on cases from the textbook so if you read that you were good to go.

People are too critical of Dr. Conroy. Yes, reading the chapter because lecture is a must, but that's because the lectures are meant to supplement and explain the readings further. I loved her class because instead of just reading the lectures slides AT us, she spoke TO us. She is engaging, and seems strict but her class isn't even hard. 10/10 prof

She wants you to teach yourself the course, very info heavy. Lectures are barely related to textbook. Assignment guidelines are confusing. Also, she's openly politically biased and happy to be so because she's "right" and is morally obligated to teach the next generation to think the way she does. Not open to questions or "incorrect" views. Yikes!!

Amy was a tough professor because she didn't teach much in the lecture and expected you to read the textbook for every lecture. Her lecture slides were more boring than watching paint dry. She is an on the clock professor and is very stingy with flexibility and constantly replies with “look at the syllabus”. She rarely showed up on time to lecture.

Dr. Conroy's lectures lacked information and she expects you to teach the material to yourself via the textbook. If you do not like reading, DO NOT take this class as without reading all chapters of the text book you won't succeed. TA's are okay but lack any communication with Dr. Conroy it seems and can't really answer questions.

Amy Conroy was one of the worst profs I've ever during my experience at SFU so far. She is not suitable to be a professor. Her lectures lack information. She basically expects her students to learn the course by themselves through the textbook.The TA's are even disconnected with her and lack information from Dr Conroy.

Dr. Conroy's course was incredibly frustrating. The assessments lacked clear instructions, and reaching out for help was nearly impossible as she and the TAs were very hard to approach and communicate with. The tutorials were the only helpful aspect, as I had to rely on classmates for support. Overall, extremely disappointing.

In-class assignments are really hard to do well on them and the ta's are very hard markers. Her slides are really hard to understand and barely have any info. Interesting course if prof was better at teaching.
She talk's really fast to the point were you cant write everything she's saying. Don't take her class..... Save your GPA

i literally emailed the prof if i could do an assignment a different day because i got DISOWNED and had to IMMEDIATELY MOVE OUT. she said no. thats all i have to say. if i could give her a 0, i would.

Fire her. She expects you to read the entire textbook and become an expert on the topic instead of teaching it herself. Lectures are no help at all as the slides are vague and she broadly explains topics from the book. Marking is insane and down to the T for an intro-level course. You could answer a Q right and she'll still mark it wrong.

Passionate prof but god are the lectures the worst stuff I have ever sat through in my life. Slides are dull with a few words at most as she talks in circles expecting us to catch every word and come out as experts in the end. Stay away, because this course is basically expected to be self taught through the textbook.

She knows what she's talking about and the tests are pretty easy, also provides a good amount of review material. However, assignment questions are unclear and she often expects you to know things that aren't specifically asked. You might think you're answering the question but she takes off marks because things are "missing"

What overlaps between her slides in the lecture and what is said in the text are guaranteed to be on the exam. She is definitely one of the more strict profs I've had but she is not unreasonable especially if you are putting in the work.
Easy class, straight forward.  Assignments super straight forward and short.  Tests were verbatim from her notes and outlines.  Easy A without opening textbook

Seemed like a new interim professor, no power point slides, just had outlines. Exams are quite difficult have to know material inside and out. And once again, NO POWERPOINTS, first time in three years a lecture did not have a power point. That's a first so if you're a visual learner like me, well tough luck.

Honestly hes a great prof. Hilarious, easy to understand and two open book exams. The final was cumulative which was fine since it was open book and his lectures were actually enjoyable. Though, he does mark hard and the problem-solving questions/assignment were quite difficult. Exam questions were straightforward and TA's were nice.

Very engaging lectures, he clearly knows what he's talking about and has an opinion on it lol. Detailed notes are critical since exams are open book. Tough grader, class average on some assignment's was quite low, need to be very detailed in written responses/assignments as his grading criteria is clearly very specific.

Slightly condescending, but he was a lawyer so. Otherwise, he's super funny and engaging in class. Keep in mind his exams are open-book, but you should prepare because the questions are very specific at times, so you need detailed, organized notes. However, he tells you exactly how to study, which is super helpful! Final kinda cooked me sigh

No textbook for this course thus everything texted is from lectures (Lectures recorded, no extra reading required). Lectures are interesting and organised. While the critical thinking assignments can be challenging (heard that class average is under 50%), you can redo them at the end of the semester and people usually do better.

Honestly he's such a fair prof. People wanna rate him low because they don't do well in his class or don't wanna study but truth is you have to put in work to pass a course like this and you can't just rate him low because you didn't get the best grade. His exams were so fair and were quite literally how he explained they would be.

he's really knowledgeable about what he teaches & explains concepts really well with entertaining examples to help you understand. tough grader but gives good resources to help you succeed & gives chances to improve ur grade. exams are open book; take good notes in class & listen to recordings to revise - if smt is important he'll emphasize it.
Most of your grade comes from 2 exams. There are no easy marks in this class; you must put the time and effort in it to get a good grade. Overall though, class and prof was fairly decent.
Went out of his way to organize and administrate one of the greatest events any professor could have ever, a true king and the realist.
I really appreciated Dr. King's attention to detail and passion in his delivery of lectures.

Ive gotta be real with yall. Ive been deadass depressed for the past four months. Its a literal nightmare. But having Cassius as a prof has made me enjoy life more than I think I ever have before. Im goina be smiling for the next two hours, bet you anything. Thank you so much Cassius.

A really great professor. You can tell he truly cares about his students and what he is teaching. Exams are difficult, but he provides lots of practise and feedback, so if you are willing to put in the time and effort it is not that hard to do well. Best professor I have had at SFU so far.
Cassius is a great prof! Super attentive to students and the right level of humour in course content. Make sure to pay attention to his postings/announcements and you'll do great
Dr. King was very helpful and easy to approach. There are times when Cassius was too busy and could not reply as quickly, but he is multitasking during the course. 135 was lit!
best prof ever

He really makes you work for your grade. I remember in his tutorial he said he was harsh on marking first year papers to help prep us for future classes. Definitely knowledgeable on the subject of law. Gave study guides for tests, which were really straight-forward. Test questions can be puzzling at times.

Russell was a great prof! His lectures were quite boring but, he tried his best to make them engaging as possible! Exams were very fair! Midterm and Final were based off of a study guide. Make sure you read your textbook for tutorial as it is 20% of your grade. Overall, good prof!!

Let's you pick which topic you want to write for your term paper. Different topics have different due dates. He makes a study guide for the class on what to study for the midterm & final. Note: some of the things on the study guide won't be on the midterm/final. He post his lecture slides a few days later after giving the lecture.
Really boring class and skips through notes really fast. attending class was not mandatory but attending the tutorial was. You must read the text.

You can tell Russell really cared about his students and making sure they understood the material! That being said, his lectures did get boring, partly due to the material and partly due to his slides being overly wordy. Midterm and final based off study guide and were extremely fair. Also, participation in tutorials was worth 20%.

Great class as he makes everything much easier to understand. Midterm was extremely fair. Easily accessible outside of class so make sure you go to him if you ever need help. Overall great course with a solid prof who cares about what he's teaching. Plus he has a lot of experience with the law so he has quite a few interesting stories.
Good
Avoid this professor if you want a boost in your GPA. Never had a harder marker.

EXTREMELY hard marker. Marked a 100 level course as if it were a 400 level course. Responds to emails well but wording is condescending. Course is nothing but writing. My advice is for you to AVOID this prof at all costs and take this class in a lecture, NOT online. Your GPA will thank you.

Helene Love was a great professor who was always answered any questions and was quick to debrief on marked work. Word counts were too short for what was required for the assignments, however the grading rubrics were clear and easy to follow. Exams were heavily focused on problem based questions but unlimited cheat sheets were allowed.

This course was unreasonably hard for a first year course. The TAs marked the assignments were very harshly and the word counts were too short given the depth they were looking for. While there was criteria for the assignments, they were still confusing and I had to consistently figure out what to do by myself, and my TA didn't clarify much.

Tbh contrary to the ratings, I didn't find Helenes lectures that long. The weekly to-dos were very reasonable (open book quiz no time limit). 3 assignments were hard to get good grade on as TAs mark rough, deducting marks a lot. But the final was reasonable and she gave clear explanation on what will be on it; 5 MC and 3 long answer.

She requires you to buy the textbook that she wrote & her lectures are 25 minutes long, expected to pretty much teach yourself the class. Lectures are super brief and touches base on some of the text you read otherwise other parts of the lecture are to watch other professors 3rd year lectures. TA's mark very hard, must solve cases like a lawyer.

Unreasonably tough grading. TAs are hard to reach. The prof has unreasonable expectations regarding assignments and quizzes and many harsh restrictions regarding assignments (i.e. word counts) and expects you to include all case law and rules within the restrictive word count. DO NOT recommend.
